1.texto_original: OR Nahas Ammonita salì, e pose campo sopra Iabes di Galaad; e tutti que’ di Iabes di Galaad dissero a Nahas: Fa’ patti con noi, e noi ti serviremo.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 11:1

"OR Nahas Ammonita salì, e pose campo sopra Iabes di Galaad; e tutti que’ di Iabes di Galaad dissero a Nahas: Fa’ patti con noi, e noi ti serviremo."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 11:1

Philological Analysis

"OR Nahas Ammonita salì, e pose campo sopra Iabes di Galaad; e tutti que’ di Iabes di Galaad dissero a Nahas: Fa’ patti con noi, e noi ti serviremo."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"OR Nahas Ammonita salì, e pose campo sopra Iabes di Galaad; e tutti que’ di Iabes di Galaad dissero a Nahas: Fa’ patti con noi, e noi ti serviremo."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

7.texto_original: E prese un paio di buoi, e li tagliò in pezzi, i quali egli mandò per tutte le contrade d’Israle, per certi messi, dicendo: Chiunque non uscirà dietro a Saulle, e dietro a Samuele, il simigliante si farà a’ suoi buoi. E lo spavento del Signore cadde sopra il popolo; e tutti uscirono fuori, come se non fossero stati che un uomo.

11.texto_original: E il giorno seguente Saulle dispose il popolo in tre schiere; e quelle entrarono dentro al campo in su la veglia della mattina, e percossero gli Ammoniti, finchè il dì fu riscaldato. E avvenne che coloro che scamparono furono dispersi qua e là, e non ne rimase di loro due insieme.

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
13.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Ma Saulle disse: Ei non si farà morire alcuno in questo giorno; perciocchè oggi il Signore ha fatta liberazione in Israele."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: E Samuele disse al popolo: Venite, andiamo in Ghilgal, e quivi rinnoviamo il reame.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 11:14

"E Samuele disse al popolo: Venite, andiamo in Ghilgal, e quivi rinnoviamo il reame."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 11:14

Philological Analysis

"E Samuele disse al popolo: Venite, andiamo in Ghilgal, e quivi rinnoviamo il reame."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

15.texto_original: E tutto il popolo andò in Ghilgal, e quivi costituirono re Saulle, davanti al Signore, in Ghilgal; quivi ancora, nel cospetto del Signore, sacrificarono sacrificii da render grazie. E Saulle, e tutti gli uomini d’Israele, si rallegrarono quivi grandemente.
15.normal:

"E tutto il popolo andò in Ghilgal, e quivi costituirono re Saulle, davanti al Signore, in Ghilgal; quivi ancora, nel cospetto del Signore, sacrificarono sacrificii da render grazie. E Saulle, e tutti gli uomini d’Israele, si rallegrarono quivi grandemente."
